A private membership

The biggest deal of your life shouldn't be your first.

Rare Company is a private membership for established UK business owners who intend to sell within the next five years — run by exited founders, alongside people who have already signed.

Request consideration £1,000 a month · Membership by application
Scroll
01

Selling is a game the other side plays every day.

When you sell, it will be your first time. The buyer will have done it many times; their advisers, hundreds. That asymmetry — not effort, not luck — is what decides most deals.

The usual answer is advisory: capable, expensive, and engaged at the end, once the decision is made and most of the outcome is already set. Advisers matter — at the right moment. But what actually changes the number starts years earlier: the shape of the business, the timing, and the judgement of owners who have already been through it.

That judgement is the one thing you cannot buy at the point of sale. So we put it in a room, years early, on your side of the table.

A membership with one job

Rare Company exists to increase the value of your business between now and the day you sell it — and to make sure that when the day comes, you're ready, priced right, and properly advised.

Not a course. Not a networking calendar. A private membership of owners heading for the same door, and exited founders who have already walked through it.

02

What membership holds.

i.The Rooms+

Quarterly · Private dinners & breakfasts · Across the UK

Exited founders at every table. No badges, no speakers selling from a stage, no notes taken. The conversations that change valuations happen over dinner, not at conferences. Once a year, we go further: a single occasion worth clearing the diary for.

ii.Your Table+

Monthly · Six to eight owners · Chaired

A standing group of owners at a similar stage and scale, meeting privately every month. The same faces each time: people with full context on your business, your numbers and your intentions, who tell you the truth because they have nothing to sell you.

iii.The Panel+

On request · Convened within 24 hours · In confidence

Sell now or in two years? Take the earn-out? Hire a managing director first? Which adviser? Send the question in, and we convene two or three members who have faced exactly that decision. A private call, no minutes kept, no fee attached.

iv.The Black Book+

Always open · Vetted specialists · Rated by members

Corporate finance, legal, tax, wealth, communications. Every name vetted, every one rated by members who have actually paid their invoices. Advisers appear in the Black Book. They do not appear at dinner.

v.The Minutes+

Monthly · Members only · In writing

A brief drawn from every room: what buyers are actually paying, where lenders are tightening, which structures are getting done, what sellers regret. Not news — what owners like you are seeing, months before it is written anywhere.

vi.Where You Stand+

At joining · Revisited every year

Membership begins with a private valuation and exit-readiness review: what your business is worth today, what is suppressing that number, and the priorities that move it. We revisit it every year, so you know precisely what membership has been worth.

03

What it costs, and what it's worth.

i.

The cost

£1,000 a month.

£12,000 a year, and that is the whole of it. No joining fee, no success fee, no percentage of your sale.

ii.

The comparison

A retainer alone: £2,000–£10,000 a month.

That is what a sell-side adviser typically costs, with a success fee on top — and it begins in the final year, when most of the outcome is already set. Rare Company is a fraction of that cost, and starts years earlier. It does not replace good advisers; it means you arrive prepared, and appoint the right ones on the right terms.

iii.

The return

£50,000 of profit → £250,000 at completion.

Businesses sell at a multiple of profit, so every improvement you make lands multiplied — fifty thousand pounds added, or defended, becomes a quarter of a million at a five-times multiple. And preparation decides whether you complete at all: most businesses that go to market never do. The prepared ones are the exception.

04

Who the room is for.

Owners and co-founders of established UK businesses — as a guide, £2 million or more in revenue — who intend to sell, in whole or in part, within the next five years. And a small number of exited founders, who sit on your side of the table for the company, not for a fee.

We keep the membership deliberately small, and we don't admit advisers, brokers, or anyone who would treat the room as a pipeline. Nothing is sold inside Rare Company, and nobody in the room wants your money. That is what makes the conversations worth having.

“Nobody in the room wants your money.”

The first thing we tell every applicant

“I spent two years assuming my business was worth what my accountant said. One dinner recalibrated that entirely.”

Member · Manufacturing, North West

“The Panel call about my earn-out was worth more than any invoice I've ever paid an adviser.”

Member · Software, London

“It's the only room I've been in where nobody wanted anything from me.”

Member · Consumer brands, Scotland

05

How a seat is offered.

You request consideration. We read everything and reply to everyone. Then we talk — about your business, your timing, and whether the room as it stands can genuinely help you. If it can, we'll offer you a seat. If the timing isn't right, we'll say so honestly, and the door stays open.

06

House rules.

  1. In confidence

    What is said in the room stays in the room. No notes, no quotes, no exceptions.

  2. Experience, not theory

    Every table is anchored by people who have done the thing, not studied it, advised on it or posted about it.

  3. No pitching

    Nothing is sold in the room. Not services, not deals, not yourself. Nobody here wants your money.

  4. By name

    There are no open tickets. Every seat is considered, and every member is there by name.

  5. Rare settings

    Private dining rooms, townhouses and tables you cannot book. The setting is part of the conversation.

07

Who sets the table.

Rare Company is run by founders who have sold. In the years before our own exits we went looking for this room — the candid second opinions, the people who had already done it — and it didn't exist. So after we signed, we built it.

Eddie Whittingham Founder · Exited

Bootstrapped and sold a SaaS business to a NASDAQ-listed company in under four years. Now builds and backs privately owned businesses through his ventures and advisory.

Astan Morarji Founder · Exited

Has built and exited companies across the UK, Europe and Asia Pacific, and taken founders from their first hires to multi-million-pound acquisition talks.

09

Questions, answered.

What does it cost?

£1,000 a month, billed annually. Many members treat it as a business expense; take your accountant's advice, not ours. There is no joining fee and no percentage of your sale.

I'm not selling for another four or five years. Too early?

That is the right time. Five years out, everything about the number is still movable — the profit, the structure, the story. A year out, your options have narrowed to the price you're offered.

I've already exited. Why would I join?

Because you hold the thing every other member needs, and the room holds what you need next: what to build, back or buy, and people who understand what the years after a sale are actually like.

Is this exit advisory?

No. Advisers are paid on the transaction; members are not paid at all. When the time comes for advisers, you will appoint better ones, on better terms — and you'll know which ones, because members rate the advisers they have actually paid.

Is it genuinely private?

Wanting to sell is the most commercially sensitive fact about your business. So confidentiality is not a feature of Rare Company; it is the precondition. No notes, no quotes, no exceptions.

How do I join?

You request consideration below. We read everything, reply to everyone, and then we talk. If the room can genuinely help you, we'll offer you a seat.
